normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem dijkstra_size {n source : Nat} {edges : List (Nat × Nat × Nat)} (hpre : ∀ e ∈ edges, let (u,v,w) := e; u < n ∧ v < n) : (dijkstra n source edges).length = n := by
  sorry
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The returned distance vector has length n.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem dijkstra_source_zero {n source : Nat} {edges : List (Nat × Nat × Nat)} (hpre : ∀ e ∈ edges, let (u,v,w) := e; u < n ∧ v < n) : (dijkstra n source edges)[source]? = some (0 : Nat) := by
  sorry
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The source distance is exactly Some(0).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem dijkstra_achievable {n source : Nat} {edges : List (Nat × Nat × Nat)} (hpre : ∀ e ∈ edges, let (u,v,w) := e; u < n ∧ v < n) {v d : Nat} (hd : (dijkstra n source edges)[v]? = some d) : ∃ p : List Nat, p ≠ [] ∧ p.head? = some source ∧ p.get? (p.length - 1) = some v ∧ (∀ i : Nat, i + 1 < p.length → let u := p.get! i; let w := p.get! (i+1); ∃ wgt : Nat, (u,w,wgt) ∈ edges) ∧ (pathWeight p edges = d) := by
  sorry
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If dist[v] = Some(d), then there exists a path from source to v with total weight d.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem dijkstra_optimal {n source : Nat} {edges : List (Nat × Nat × Nat)} (hpre : ∀ e ∈ edges, let (u,v,w) := e; u < n ∧ v < n) {v d : Nat} (hd : (dijkstra n source edges)[v]? = some d) : ∀ d' : Nat, (∃ p : List Nat, p ≠ [] ∧ p.head? = some source ∧ p.get? (p.length - 1) = some v ∧ pathWeight p edges = d') → ¬ (d' < d) := by
  sorry
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If dist[v] = Some(d), no path from source to v has total weight less than d.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem dijkstra_unreachable {n source : Nat} {edges : List (Nat × Nat × Nat)} (hpre : ∀ e ∈ edges, let (u,v,w) := e; u < n ∧ v < n) {v : Nat} (hv : (dijkstra n source edges)[v]? = none) : ¬ (∃ p : List Nat, p ≠ [] ∧ p.head? = some source ∧ p.get? (p.length - 1) = some v ∧ (∃ d : Nat, pathWeight p edges = d)) := by
  sorry
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If dist[v] = None, then there is no path from source to v.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem dijkstra_terminates {n source : Nat} {edges : List (Nat × Nat × Nat)} (hpre : ∀ e ∈ edges, let (u,v,w) := e; u < n ∧ v < n) : Termination (dijkstra n source edges) := by
  sorry
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Dijkstra terminates on every valid input.
